Misconfigurations.Title: Root file system is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root file system prevents applications from writing to their local disk. This can limit intrusions, as attackers will not be able to tamper with the file system or write foreign executables to disk.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'inflate' of Deployment 'inflate' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Change 'containers[].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to 'true'.

Misconfigurations.Title: Default security context configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: container inflate in default namespace is using the default security context
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Title: Default security context configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: deployment inflate in default namespace is using the default security context, which allows root privileges
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: Instance with unencrypted block device.
Misconfigurations.Description: Block devices should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is held securely at rest.
Misconfigurations.Message: Root block device is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on encryption for all block devices

Misconfigurations.Title: Instance with unencrypted block device.
Misconfigurations.Description: Block devices should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is held securely at rest.
Misconfigurations.Message: Root block device is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on encryption for all block devices

Misconfigurations.Title: Root file system is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root file system prevents applications from writing to their local disk. This can limit intrusions, as attackers will not be able to tamper with the file system or write foreign executables to disk.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'inflate' of Deployment 'inflate' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Change 'containers[].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to 'true'.

Misconfigurations.Title: EKS Clusters should have the public access disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: EKS clusters are available publicly by default, this should be explicitly disabled in the vpc_config of the EKS cluster resource.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public cluster access is enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Don't enable public access to EKS Clusters

Misconfigurations.Title: EKS cluster should not have open CIDR range for public access
Misconfigurations.Description: EKS Clusters have public access cidrs set to 0.0.0.0/0 by default which is wide open to the internet. This should be explicitly set to a more specific private CIDR range
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster allows access from a public CIDR: 0.0.0.0/0
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Don't enable public access to EKS Clusters

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range
